model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The schedule and optimizer are already aligned to the fixed-step budget, so the next low-risk gain is a modest capacity bump in the compact CNN itself. A slightly wider feature stack and classifier should improve fit without changing the training loop or adding complexity.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"primary_mode":"indexing","declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"],"rationale":"The wider model step clearly regressed, so the next move is to restore the previous compact capacity and make a conservative optimizer update that is better suited to short fixed-step training. AdamW with slightly lower learning rate and light decay should stabilize the already-good schedule without adding much complexity.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["indexing","topk"],"rationale":"The last regression came from the optimizer change, not from the compact CNN family itself. Restore the proven Adam schedule and test whether a modest width bump can buy extra validation loss reduction once the dynamics are back in the good regime.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["layout"],"rationale":"The wider model regressed twice, so the safest next move is to return to the compact capacity that already produced the best visible loss and only refine the step size. A slightly lower Adam learning rate may improve the fixed-step cosine run without adding any training-loop complexity.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The compact two-block model seems to have plateaued around the current loss level, so the next useful experiment is to add one more feature-extraction stage while restoring the stronger learning rate. That keeps the training loop unchanged and tests whether a deeper hierarchy can use the fixed-step budget better than extra width did.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 2","new":"DEPTH = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The depth-3 compact CNN is now clearly in a better region, so the next cheap gain is a modest width increase on top of the already-working optimizer and schedule. This keeps the same training dynamics while giving the feature extractor and head a little more capacity to use the fixed-step budget.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The current 3-block model is still in a compact-underfit regime, so the next clean test is one more downsampling stage with a slightly larger head. That keeps the proven optimizer and schedule unchanged while giving the network more hierarchical capacity.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 3","new":"DEPTH = 4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The depth-4 backbone is working, and the remaining loss level still looks capacity-limited rather than optimization-limited. A modest width increase in both the convolutional stem and the classifier is the lowest-risk next way to see if the model can use the fixed-step budget better.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"micro","declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The architecture is already the strongest region so far; the remaining low-risk lever is to feed more data into each of the fixed 256 updates. Doubling the batch size should reduce gradient noise and may let the current model extract a bit more from the same step budget without changing the successful network shape.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"indexing","declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The large-batch run already pushed the model close to 1.0 loss, so the remaining headroom is more likely in Adam's adaptation dynamics than in the architecture. Lowering beta2 should make the optimizer react faster over this short fixed-step horizon without disturbing the working batch-size change.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"micro","declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The beta2 reduction clearly hurt, so first restore the good Adam setting and then test a slightly larger batch. With the model already near 1.0 loss, the remaining headroom is likely from squeezing more signal out of each of the fixed-step updates rather than changing the architecture again.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The current run is finally in a regime where the model is large enough that a little explicit regularization may help instead of hurt. A small Adam weight decay is the least disruptive way to test whether the remaining validation-loss gap is now generalization rather than optimization.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The last run showed that the large model is now starting to benefit from regularization, not just capacity. A slightly stronger Adam weight decay is the most conservative next test for squeezing the validation loss down without changing the architecture or the stable large-batch setup.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"The run is already near the best visible region, so the remaining gain is more likely a small step-size mismatch than a missing architectural change. Lowering the learning rate slightly should smooth the final fixed-step updates while preserving the beneficial regularization setup.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"micro","declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk","caching"],"rationale":"The last loss bump looks like a step-size/regulation mismatch, not an architecture issue, so restore the optimizer settings that were already working and test a slightly larger batch. That keeps the successful depth-4 backbone intact while giving each fixed-step update a bit more data to work with.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 448","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"indexing","declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The current setup is already near the time ceiling, so the next move should not increase compute. A slightly less inert Adam second-moment setting is the cheapest remaining way to try to sharpen the last fixed-step updates in this large-batch regime.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The beta2 tweak clearly missed, so restore the better-adapted Adam state and make only a tiny step-size refinement. At this stage the model is already well regularized and near the time ceiling, so a conservative LR nudge is the lowest-risk way to try to beat the current loss without changing the rest of the successful setup.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.9e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"micro","declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The last two tweaks moved away from the best observed setting: batch 448 and LR 1.9e-3 both underperformed the earlier 384/2e-3 run. Reverting the batch size to the proven 384 is the simplest remaining change most likely to recover or slightly beat the current best loss without destabilizing the working model.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 448","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The last change understepped the learning rate and lost the best region. Reverting to the previously successful 2e-3 setting is the safest final move because it matches the run that produced the strongest visible loss while keeping the rest of the stable large-batch setup unchanged.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.9e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}
